acceptance_criteria: Documentation and knowledge transfer are sufficient for maintenance.
Security and compliance requirements for Public Sector are satisfied.
CI/CD and operational monitoring are in place and validated.
Solution meets functional requirements and agreed scope.
Performance and reliability targets are met under expected load.
business_context: In a rapidly evolving market, Good-Thomas aims to strengthen capabilities in the Public Sector domain. This initiative will modernize key workflows and improve operational efficiency, with a focus on DevOps Automation.
deliverables: Working MVP/prototype suitable for stakeholder review.
Project plan and delivery roadmap for DevOps Automation.
Architecture design and system documentation for the Public Sector context.
Final deployment package ready for production usage.
Operational runbooks, monitoring dashboards, and maintenance procedures.
evaluation_process: Proposals will be reviewed by a committee from Good-Thomas. Evaluation will include technical expertise, project methodology, team qualifications, delivery timelines, and cost-effectiveness. Shortlisted vendors may be invited for follow-up discussions.
executive_summary: Good-Thomas invites qualified vendors to propose an implementation for "Request for Proposal: DevOps Automation - Public Sector". This RFP summarizes requirements, expectations, and proposal guidelines. Project type: DevOps Automation. Duration: 6 months. Team size: 5. Budget: $250K - $500K. Start: April 12, 2026. Location: Warsaw, PL (Remote Work Allowed).

milestones.description: Produce architecture, data flows, and technical decisions for Public Sector.

milestones.title: Kickoff & Discovery

milestones.description: Support UAT and address feedback to meet acceptance criteria.
milestones.title: Integration & Hardening

milestones.description: Deploy to production and hand over documentation and operational materials.
milestones.title: Final Deployment

milestones.description: Integrate key components, address performance/reliability, and stabilize delivery.
milestones.title: MVP Delivery

objectives: Deliver a maintainable architecture and clear documentation suitable for enterprise usage.
Implement best practices and a robust delivery process for DevOps Automation.
Ensure observability, reliability, and performance under expected load.
Provide knowledge transfer and enable internal teams to operate the system post-delivery.
proposal_submission_guidelines: Commercial proposal with assumptions and breakdown.
Proposed methodology, delivery approach, and timeline.
Company profile and relevant project references.
